responsibilities: Design and develop high-fidelity robot simulation environments that accurately model sensor, actuator, and physics behavior
Build digital twin systems that mirror real-world robot hardware to accelerate software testing and reduce physical testing costs
Implement sensor simulation models including cameras, LiDAR, and IMUs to generate realistic synthetic data for perception training
Develop and tune physics engine parameters to ensure simulated robot dynamics closely match real-world hardware behavior
Create procedurally generated environments and test scenarios to enable scalable, automated validation of robot software
Collaborate with perception and controls teams to integrate simulation environments into continuous integration and testing pipelines
Optimize simulation performance using GPU acceleration to support large-scale parallel training and testing workloads
Validate sim-to-real transfer by comparing simulated and real robot performance and iteratively refining simulation fidelity
Build tooling and APIs that allow researchers and engineers to easily configure, run, and analyze simulation experiments
